'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Nesaku no Kami) * {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami) * {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=}} (Mikahayahi no Kami) * {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami) * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami * {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami) * {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami) These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld. == History == [[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest. Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers. == Annual Events == * **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival * **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival * **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession) * **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai) == Access == * Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan * 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]] * Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurugijinja.jp Official website of Tsurugi Shrine] (in Japanese) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (直方市)|version=102200157|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%9B%B4%E6%96%B9%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Nogata)]] [[Category:Buildings in Nogata, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nogata]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:劔神社 (直方市)]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

{{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神, 根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ}}, {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=}}, {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}}, {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}}, {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} |Rank = Former {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |annual festival = |map = {{location map|Japan Fukuoka|label=Tsurugi Shrine|coordinates={{coord2|33|45|24.2|N|130|42|10.2|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|45|24.2|N|130|42|10.2|E}}}} }} '''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Nesaku no Kami) * {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami) * {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=}} (Mikahayahi no Kami) * {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami) * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami * {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami) * {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami) These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld. == History == [[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest. Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers. == Annual Events == * **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival * **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival * **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession) * **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai) == Access == * Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan * 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]] * Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurugijinja.jp Official website of Tsurugi Shrine] (in Japanese) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (直方市)|version=102200157|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%9B%B4%E6%96%B9%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Nogata)]] [[Category:Buildings in Nogata, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nogata]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:劔神社 (直方市)]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system. == Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Nesaku no Kami) * {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami) * {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=}} (Mikahayahi no Kami) * {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami) * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami * {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami) * {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami) These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld. == History == [[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]] The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest. Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers. == Annual Events == * **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival * **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival * **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession) * **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai) == Access == * Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan * 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]] * Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]] == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * [http://www.tsurugijinja.jp Official website of Tsurugi Shrine] (in Japanese) {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|劔神社 (直方市)|version=102200157|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Mononobe clan]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[sn:E5%8A%94%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE_(%E7%9B%B4%E6%96%B9%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Tsurugi Shrine (Nogata)]] [[Category:Buildings in Nogata, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nogata]] [[Category:Son-sha in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[ja:劔神社 (直方市)]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Nesaku no Kami) * {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami) * {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=}} (Mikahayahi no Kami) * {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami) * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami * {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami) * {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)
These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.
== History == [[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]
The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.
Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.
== Annual Events == * **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival * **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival * **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession) * **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)
== Access == * Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan * 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]] * Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Nesaku no Kami) * {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami) * {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=}} (Mikahayahi no Kami) * {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami) * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami * {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami) * {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)
These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.
== History == [[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]
The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.
Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.
== Annual Events == * **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival * **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival * **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession) * **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)
== Access == * Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan * 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]] * Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsurugijinja.jp Official website of Tsurugi Shrine] (in Japanese)

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}} * {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク}} (Nesaku no Kami) * {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami) * {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=}} (Mikahayahi no Kami) * {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami) * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami * {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami) * {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)
These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.
== History == [[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]
The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.
Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.
== Annual Events == * **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival * **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival * **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession) * **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)
== Access == * Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan * 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]] * Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External Links == * [http://www.tsurugijinja.jp Official website of Tsurugi Shrine] (in Japanese)

During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.

Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Nesaku no Kami) * {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwatsutsunoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9010200}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami) * {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=|1=Mikahayahi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574224}} (Mikahayahi no Kami) * {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami) * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami * {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami) * {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)
These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.
== History == [[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]
The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国|1=Tsukushi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206461}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.
Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.
== Annual Events == * **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival * **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival * **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession) * **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)
== Access == * Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan * 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]] * Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
* {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Nesaku no Kami)
* {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwatsutsunoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9010200}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami)
* {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=|1=Mikahayahi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574224}} (Mikahayahi no Kami)
* {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami)
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami
* {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami)
* {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)

These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.

== History ==
[[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]

The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国|1=Tsukushi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206461}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.

Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."

In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.

== Annual Events ==
* **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival
* **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival
* **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession)
* **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)

== Access ==
* Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan
* 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]]
* Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop
* 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
* {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Nesaku no Kami)
* {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwatsutsunoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9010200}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami)
* {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|qq=|1=Mikahayahi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574224}} (Mikahayahi no Kami)
* {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami)
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami
* {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami)
* {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)

These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.

== History ==
[[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]

The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国|1=Tsukushi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206461}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.

Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|qq=|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|qq=|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."

In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|qq=|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.

== Annual Events ==
* **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival
* **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival
* **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession)
* **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)

== Access ==
* Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan
* 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]]
* Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop
* 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|qq=|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
* {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Nesaku no Kami)
* {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwatsutsunoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9010200}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami)
* {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|1=Mikahayahi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574224}} (Mikahayahi no Kami)
* {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami)
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami
* {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami)
* {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)

These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.

== History ==
[[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]

The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国|1=Tsukushi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206461}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.

Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."

In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.

== Annual Events ==
* **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival
* **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival
* **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession)
* **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)

== Access ==
* Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan
* 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]]
* Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop
* 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.

* {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami)
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami
* {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami)
* {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)

These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.

== History ==
[[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]

The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国|1=Tsukushi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206461}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.

Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."

In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.

== Annual Events ==
* **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival
* **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival
* **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession)
* **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)

== Access ==
* Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan
* 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]]
* Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop
* 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.

In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."

In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.

== Annual Events ==
* **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival
* **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival
* **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession)
* **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)

== Access ==
* Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan
* 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]]
* Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop
* 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
* {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Nesaku no Kami)
* {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwatsutsunoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9010200}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami)
* {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|1=Mikahayahi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574224}} (Mikahayahi no Kami)
* {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami)
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami
* {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami)
* {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)

These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.

== History ==
[[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]

The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国|1=Tsukushi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206461}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.

Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."

In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.

== Annual Events ==
* **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival
* **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival
* **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession)
* **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)

== Access ==
* Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan
* 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]]
* Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop
* 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}}
* {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}}
* {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}
* {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Nesaku no Kami)
* {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwatsutsunoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9010200}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami)
* {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|1=Mikahayahi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574224}} (Mikahayahi no Kami)
* {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami)
* {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami
* {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami)
* {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)

These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.

== History ==
[[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]

The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国|1=Tsukushi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206461}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.

Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."

In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.

== Annual Events ==
* **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival
* **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival
* **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession)
* **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)

== Access ==
* Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan
* 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]]
* Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop
* 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]

'''Tsurugi Shrine''' (劔神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Nōgata, Fukuoka|Nogata]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], Japan. It holds the former shrine Rank of {{ill|Village shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the pre-modern shrine Ranking system.
== Enshrined Deities == * {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} * {{ill|Izanami|ja|伊邪那美大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナミ|1=Izanami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q682306}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}} * {{ill|Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja|石折神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwasaku and Nesaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Iwasaku no Kami), {{ill|Nesaku|ja|根折神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nesaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワサク・ネサク|1=Iwasaku and Nesaku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11287276}} (Nesaku no Kami) * {{ill|Iwatatsuno-o|ja|石筒之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwatatsuno-o|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イワツツノオ|1=Iwatsutsunoo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9010200}} (Iwatatsuno-o no Kami) * {{ill|Mikahayahi|ja|甕速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikahayahi|1=Mikahayahi-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11574224}} (Mikahayahi no Kami) * {{ill|Hihayahino-kami|ja|樋速日神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hihayahino-kami|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Hihayahino-kami) * {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|建御雷之男神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミカヅチ}} (Takemikazuchi no Kami), also known as Takefutsu no Kami and Toyofutsu no Kami * {{ill|Kuraokami|ja|闇淤加美神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuraokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淤加美神}} (Kuraokami no Kami) * {{ill|Kuramitsuha|ja|闇御津羽神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuramitsuha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クラミツハ}} (Kuramitsuha no Kami)
These deities are believed to have been formed from the blood that dripped from the sword during the purification of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|伊邪那岐大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izanagi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to イザナギ}} after returning from the underworld.
== History == [[File:tsurugi003.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Torii gate]]
The shrine's origins are attributed to {{ill|Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja|田道命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tadomichi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the first {{ill|Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|筑紫国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukushi no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 筑紫国|1=Tsukushi Province|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1206461}}, who is said to have led the Tsukushi Mononobe clan in worshiping the deities. His descendant Nagatahiko served as the shrine priest.
Originally called "Kuraji Daimyōjin," the shrine was located atop Mount Tenjō-dake in the Rokkatake range. During the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]], [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshimitsu|Ashikaga Yoshimitsu]] ordered the reconstruction of the shrine. In the {{ill|Sengoku period|ja|戦国時代|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengoku period|1=Sengoku Period (Japan)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q117415390}}, the local lord of Ryūgatake Castle, the {{ill|Sugi clan|ja|杉氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugi clan|1=Sugi clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11522183}}, revered the shrine and enshrined {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}, renaming it "Yatsurugi Ōkami."
In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], after a fire destroyed the shrine, it was relocated to its current site on "Kameoka." The shrine was rebuilt under the patronage of Lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagakiyo|ja|黒田長清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nagakiyo|1=Kuroda Nagakiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678705}}. The current shrine structure was established with the support of local worshippers.
== Annual Events == * **April 18:** Spring Grand Festival * **July 29:** Summer Purification Festival * **October (Sunday):** Autumn Grand Festival (every 5 years, features a sacred procession) * **December 18:** Harvest Festival (Niiname-sai)
== Access == * Located at 2565 Shimo-Shinnyū, Nogata City, Fukuoka Prefecture, Japan * 15-minute walk from JR [[:en:Shinnyū Station|Shinnyū Station]] * Near Nishitetsu Bus "Shinnyū Honmura" bus stop * 5-minute drive from {{ill|Kurate Interchange|ja|鞍手インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurate Interchange|1=Kurate interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575395}} on the [[:en:Kyushu Expressway|Kyushu Expressway]]
